pick.rationale: Arkansas earns a narrow edge in what projects as a classic toss-up game between two elite offensive teams. The Razorbacks' slightly higher-ranked offense, combined with their experience in close SEC games, should be enough to overcome Wisconsin's balanced attack. In a high-scoring affair where both teams will score consistently, Arkansas's ability to create a few more key stops or clutch baskets will be the difference in a tight contest that could go either way.

reasoningStep.title: Wisconsin vs Arkansas Round of 32 Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Analyzed team ratings, injury context, and external predictions for a closely matched 4-5 seed game, determining Arkansas has a slight edge due to offensive efficiency and tournament momentum.
evidence: Ratings show near-identical profiles: Arkansas (Torvik 20, KenPom 17) with adjOE rank 5; Wisconsin (Torvik 21, KenPom 22) with adjOE rank 8. Both defenses rank 48-50. No definitive major injury reports for either team entering Round of 32. External predictions highlight this as one of the tournament's best games, with some analysts favoring Arkansas's tournament experience under John Calipari to advance in a close contest.
